target: Architectural split — pre-compute Priority 3 change-detection candidates in a deterministic Node script run by integrity; have curmudgeon read the precomputed list instead of scanning ~462 review files in-LLM each cycle.
title: Move expensive full-drift scan from curmudgeon's hot path to integrity's cold path; curmudgeon reads pre-computed candidate list
problem: Curmudgeon's Priority 3 change-detection currently rescans every review fingerprint in-LLM each run. Empirical state of the corpus this run: 464 review files, 248 distinct review items, of which 69 are WIN-NNN and ~113 are section-style; only ~69 WIN items carry a canonical comparable fingerprint (40 pre-tldr + 15 post-tldr + 14 with bespoke shape). The Opus LLM reads (or has to scan past) the full corpus to build the drift map, then picks one item, then exits. At q4h cadence × ~6 runs/day, the same expensive scan repeats — and most repeats produce the same head-of-list candidate because drift state changes slowly relative to scan cost. The work is deterministic Node-script work being done by an LLM. Operator's framing: 'efficient curmudgeon where the cost matters and an occasionally expensive integrity.' This PROP designs the split.

scope.in_scope: Priority 3 change-detection input side. Curmudgeon reads a precomputed candidate list (cheap, every run); integrity periodically refreshes it via deterministic Node script (occasional batch cost). All review-procedure logic (Steps 5-8 of curmudgeon's per-item review) is unchanged. Fallback to existing in-prompt scan when the list is missing/stale is non-negotiable.
scope.out_of_scope: Priority 1 (queue), Priority 2 (human notes), Priority 4 (holistic), Priority 5 (spot-check). The fingerprint comparison rule itself (PROP-019 reduced-set, common-fields-only). The drift-magnitude tiebreak (commit 71be960). The decider, digest pipeline, or any consumer of curmudgeon's reviews.
explicit_non_goals_per_directive: DO NOT change the actual review procedure (Steps 5-8 of Priority 3).
DO NOT change priority queue or decider routing.
DO NOT propose a real-time watch/notify mechanism — periodic batch audit is sufficient.
DO NOT broaden scope to other curmudgeon optimizations (holistic batching, etc.).

deliverable_1_candidate_list_schema.path_argument: Operator's file-ownership convention is 'file in the writer's directory.' Integrity is the writer; curmudgeon is the reader. Existing precedent in monitor/integrity/: report-*.json, workspace-sync-skips.jsonl, narrative-cite-audit-*.json, INTEGRITY-INCIDENT-*.json — all integrity outputs. Curmudgeon already reads from many directories (monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue.json, monitor/curmudgeon/human-notes.json, monitor/curmudgeon/tracker.json, monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/*); adding monitor/integrity/drift-audit.json is no different. The 'reader's directory for readability' counter-argument is weak — it's a single read in Step 1 of Priority 3. Choose integrity/.

schema.generated_at: ISO timestamp when the audit ran
schema.generator_run_id: string identifying the integrity run that produced this list (e.g., integrity-2026-05-05T0900Z)

source_data_versions.wins_sha: short SHA of data/wins.json contents at audit time (sha256 or similar)
source_data_versions.sections_sha: short SHA of data/sections.json contents at audit time

rules_applied: PROP-019 reduced-set fingerprint compare (common fields only)
verdict-change and new-item buckets included unconditionally (above the cap)
drifting candidates ranked by max_drift_pct descending

fields_added_vs_directive_suggestion: schema_version (forward compatibility — bump if shape changes)
generator (script identity, useful for tinker audit)
verdict_changed boolean per candidate (avoids re-deriving from priority_bucket)
in_priority_queue / queue_id (answers Q5 directly inside the candidate)
fields_dropped_vs_directive_suggestion: None. Directive's suggested shape is preserved; the additions above are non-breaking.
deliberate_priority_buckets: verdict_changed — verdict field differs between current and last_review fingerprint. Highest priority. Unconditionally included regardless of cap.
new_item — item exists in wins.json/sections.json but has no prior review file. Unconditionally included regardless of cap.
large_rewrite — at least one common-field length drifted >20%. Capped portion of list; ranked by max_drift_pct descending.

tldr_only — only tldr_evidence_length or tldr_verdict_length drifted >20% AND both fields are present in BOTH fingerprints. Lowest priority among drifters; included only if cap not yet reached. Per PROP-019 §4: a pre-tldr-schema item that flags via legacy-field drift is NEVER classified as tldr_only — it's classified by the legacy field that drifted (large_rewrite or verdict_changed).

deliverable_2_integrity_prompt_edit.placement: Add as Section 7g (Drift Audit) — sits between existing 7f (Broken Curmudgeon Review Files) and Section 8 (Project Documentation). Rationale: 7-series is curmudgeon-pipeline observation; 8 onward is documentation/build. 7g extends the curmudgeon-side observation set with a deterministic candidate-list refresh.

step_diff_sketch.notes_on_diff: Single new section, ~30 lines of prose plus a code block. No edits to existing integrity steps. The audit step is gated by the 72h freshness check so most integrity runs (which fire daily) refresh on a 3-day cadence.

deliverable_2_integrity_prompt_edit.cadence_argument: Operator's lean is 3-4 days; the recommendation is **>72-hour gate, absorbed into integrity's existing daily 9am run.** Effective cadence: ~3 days. Why a gate rather than a hardcoded weekday: (a) integrity sometimes skips a day for environmental reasons (auth/disk), and a gate self-heals on the next successful run; (b) hardcoded Mon/Thu would create a 'Monday morning candidate list is 4 days old' edge case; (c) the gate degrades gracefully if integrity is down — curmudgeon falls back to in-prompt scan. Counter-argument considered: a stricter 24-hour gate would make the list always-fresh at marginal cost (~5s/day). Rejected because the audit's value isn't real-time freshness — drift state changes slowly, and curmudgeon's OBE-skip cross-check (deliverable 4) handles same-day staleness anyway. **Recommend 72h gate, document the override toggle for tinker if a faster cadence is later wanted.**

deliverable_3_curmudgeon_prompt_edit.section: Change Detection Procedure (Priority 3) — Steps 1-2 are what gets replaced.

diff_sketch.before_steps_1_through_4:
1. **Load your most recent review fingerprints.** For every item you've reviewed, the review JSON contains a `text_fingerprint` with field lengths and verdict. Build a map of `item_id → {fingerprint, reviewed_at}`.

2. **Compare against current data.** Read the current `wins.json` and `sections.json` from the fresh clone. For each item, compute the current fingerprint (same fields...). Compare against your stored fingerprint **on the fields that exist in both**. ... [PROP-019 reduced-set rule]

3. **Flag changed items.** [criteria for >20% drift, verdict change, new item, tldr-with-both]

4. **Prioritize.** Among flagged items, prefer: verdict changes > large text rewrites > new items > TLDR-only changes. **Within the 'large text rewrites' bucket, prefer the item with the largest drift magnitude...**
diff_sketch.after_steps_1_through_4:
1. **Load the precomputed candidate list.** Read `monitor/integrity/drift-audit.json` (the file lives in integrity's output directory and is refreshed by integrity's daily run per PROP-020 §7g). The list is a JSON object with a `candidates` array, each entry carrying `item_id`, `current_fingerprint`, `last_review` (or null for new items), `drifts`, `priority_bucket`, `is_pre_tldr_schema`, `verdict_changed`, `tiebreak_score`, and `in_priority_queue`. Candidates are pre-sorted: verdict_changed and new_item entries come first (highest priority), then large_rewrite entries ranked by `max_drift_pct` descending, then tldr_only entries.

   **Fallback rule (non-negotiable):** if `monitor/integrity/drift-audit.json` is missing, malformed, or its `generated_at` is more than 7 days old (`thresholds.freshness_hours` × 1.4), DO NOT use the list. Fall back to the in-prompt scan procedure described in §1f below — read all review fingerprints from `monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/`, recompute drifts inline, prioritize, and pick. Record `"used_fallback": true` in your review's `change_summary`.

2. **Cross-check the head of the candidate list against current review state.** For the top candidate `c`, look up the most recent review file for `c.item_id` in `${WORKSPACE}/monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/`. If a review exists with `reviewed_at` newer than `c.last_review.reviewed_at`, the candidate is OBE (obsolete on examination — already re-reviewed since the audit) and you skip it. Move to the next candidate. **OBE-skip cap: 3.** After 3 consecutive OBE-skips, fall through to §1f in-prompt scan rather than continuing down the list — repeated OBE-skips mean the list is significantly stale, and a fresh scan is cheaper than continuing to validate a stale list.

3. **Pick.** The first non-OBE candidate is your pick. Record in your review: `change_summary` names the field that drove the flag (e.g., 'detail_verdict_length grew from 123 to 1154 (+838%)' — see PROP-019 audit-hint guidance), `trigger: "change-detected"`, `drift_audit_generated_at: <list's generated_at>`, `drift_audit_candidate_index: <0-based index in the list>`, and (if applicable) `obe_skips: <count of items skipped>`.

4. **Prioritize when ties exist.** The list is pre-sorted, so ties have already been broken by the script. If two candidates carry identical priority_bucket and tiebreak_score (rare), prefer WINs over sections (existing tiebreak rule). Do NOT re-prioritize the list locally; trust the precomputation.

   **§1f — In-prompt fallback procedure (used only when the candidate list is missing/stale or 3 OBE-skips have fired):** Execute the original Steps 1-4 from before this PROP — load all review fingerprints, compute drifts inline against current wins.json/sections.json applying PROP-019 reduced-set rules, prioritize per existing bucket order, prefer largest magnitude within the large_rewrite bucket. This preserves correctness if integrity is broken or the list is too stale to trust. The fallback is structurally identical to the pre-PROP-020 procedure; it exists only as a degraded-mode safety net.
diff_sketch.notes_on_diff: The replacement preserves all PROP-019 logic and the magnitude tiebreak (encoded into the script's sort). The mention of `is_pre_tldr_schema` carries forward from the script's classification. The §1f fallback block is the directive's non-negotiable correctness guard.

additional_minor_edits: Step 6 (If nothing changed → fall through to holistic) is unchanged but slightly reworded: 'If the candidate list is empty AND fallback scan found no drift, fall through to Priority 4 (holistic review).'
The Important paragraph at the end of Change Detection (about decider-pushed changes vs. organic drift) is unchanged.

obe_skip_policy.argument_for_3: With a top-30 cap and a 72h gate, the candidate list is at most 72h old. In that window, curmudgeon at q4h fires up to 18 times. Realistically only 1-3 candidates will be re-reviewed mid-window (the very front of the list). After 3 OBE-skips, we have evidence that the list is significantly stale — falling through to fresh in-prompt scan is cheaper than continuing to look up review files for items we already know are stale-from-a-stale-list. This number is tunable; if operations show 3 is too tight (e.g., we keep falling through after recent integrity runs), bump to 5.
obe_skip_policy.counter_argument: If many decider patches landed and were curmudgeon-reviewed in rapid succession (e.g., the queue drained 5-6 items recently), the top of the candidate list could be all-OBE while items 7+ are still valid. Falling through after 3 wastes the in-prompt scan when the 4th item would have been the pick. **Mitigation:** the script orders by priority bucket then magnitude — verdict-changes and new-items are at the top, and these are very unlikely to be OBE (only a handful per 72h window). Also: the in-prompt fallback IS the procedure that would have run pre-PROP-020, so 'falling through' costs nothing relative to the baseline.
obe_skip_policy.alternative_considered: Instead of a hard cap, scan the entire list for OBE before picking — but this defeats the cost optimization. Rejected.
obe_skip_policy.alternative_considered_2: Cap at 5 — slightly safer but at the cost of more lookups before falling through. Reasonable; defer to operator if 3 proves tight in practice.

deliverable_4_staleness_handling.obe_lookup_mechanism: For each candidate, the curmudgeon reads `${WORKSPACE}/monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/` and finds the latest cycle file matching `item_id` (filename prefix match). It compares that file's `reviewed_at` to the candidate's `last_review.reviewed_at`. If newer → OBE. The lookup is ~10ms per item (file stat + JSON parse for one file). 3 lookups × 10ms = 30ms; trivial.

candidate_list_failure_modes.missing_file: Curmudgeon proceeds to §1f in-prompt fallback. Records `used_fallback: true` and `fallback_reason: "drift-audit.json missing"` in change_summary.
candidate_list_failure_modes.malformed_json: Curmudgeon catches the parse error, records `used_fallback: true` and `fallback_reason: "drift-audit.json malformed"`, proceeds to fallback. Integrity should detect this on its next run and either regenerate or escalate.
candidate_list_failure_modes.stale_>168h: Curmudgeon proceeds to fallback with `fallback_reason: "drift-audit.json older than 7 days (168h)"`. The 7d threshold is 2.3× the 72h gate — clearly broken integrity if we're past 7d.
candidate_list_failure_modes.wins_sha_mismatch: If the candidate list's `source_data_versions.wins_sha` doesn't match the current wins.json, fall back. The data has shifted since the audit was generated. **However**: this is conservative — most data drift since the audit will only ADD candidates (not invalidate existing ones), so a tunable option is to log a warning and proceed with the existing list while marking `data_drift_since_audit: true`. Recommend the conservative fallback for the initial implementation; revisit if it proves over-cautious.
candidate_list_failure_modes.empty_candidates_array: Treated as 'no drift detected' — fall through to Priority 4 (holistic review). NOT a fallback trigger. The audit ran, found nothing, that's a valid result.

deliverable_4_staleness_handling.integrity_failure_logging: When integrity's audit step fails (script crashed, returned non-zero, etc.), integrity emits a Major finding in its report. Curmudgeon does NOT need to detect integrity failure directly — it simply observes the file is missing/stale and falls back. This separates concerns: integrity owns 'is the audit fresh?', curmudgeon owns 'how do I act on the audit (or its absence)?'.

deliverable_5_script_design.rationale: monitor/scripts/ already exists as a directory for utility scripts (audit-narrative-citations.js, verify-pending-state.js). This belongs there. Build-scripts/ is reserved for build-pipeline scripts (digest-reviews.js, generate-html.js, etc.).
responsibilities: Read data/sections.json (canonical section data) — but in v1 only WINs are scored (see scope_in_v1 below).
Read every file in monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/, dedupe to latest cycle per base item_id (same logic as digest-reviews.js).

For each item, extract the canonical fingerprint from the latest review (skip items whose latest review fingerprint lacks the canonical claim_length / detail_evidence_length / detail_verdict_length keys — these are bespoke fingerprints that cannot be drift-compared).
Compute current fingerprint from data/wins.json (claim/finding/detail_evidence/detail_verdict_text/tldr_evidence/tldr_verdict character lengths + verdict).

Compare per PROP-019 reduced-set rule: drift = (current - old) / old × 100, computed only on fields that exist in BOTH fingerprints. Schema additions are NOT a drift signal. Same logic the prompt now spells out.
Classify each item into one of: verdict_changed, new_item, large_rewrite, tldr_only, no_drift.
Sort: verdict_changed first (no_inner_sort, alphabetical by item_id for determinism), then new_item (alpha), then large_rewrite by max_drift_pct desc, then tldr_only by max_drift_pct desc.
Cap large_rewrite + tldr_only at thresholds.top_n_cap (30). verdict_changed and new_item are uncapped.
Cross-reference monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue.json: for each candidate, set in_priority_queue=true and queue_id if the queue contains a matching target_id.
Write monitor/integrity/drift-audit.json with the full schema (see deliverable 1).
deliverable_5_script_design.scope_in_v1: WIN-NNN items only. Section items (part1/part2/...), EXP-NNN proposal reviews, ISS-NNN, HOLISTIC-* reviews are NOT scored in v1 because their latest-review fingerprints are bespoke and have no stable comparable shape. The script counts and reports them in stats.items_skipped_no_comparable_fingerprint. Phase 2 extension would add a canonical section fingerprint shape (separate PROP).
input_assumptions: Reviews directory uses the (.cN) cycle suffix convention.

WIN review fingerprints, when present in canonical shape, contain claim_length, finding_length, detail_evidence_length, detail_verdict_length, optionally tldr_evidence_length and tldr_verdict_length, and verdict.
wins.json contains id, claim, finding, detail_evidence, detail_verdict_text, tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict, verdict. (Verified: this is the current shape.)
data/wins.json and data/sections.json are committed and current (the script runs on the same checkout as integrity's other steps).
deliverable_5_script_design.output_determinism: Given identical inputs, the script produces byte-identical output. Achieved by: (a) sort all internal arrays by deterministic key, (b) use JSON.stringify with stable key order (alphabetical via Object.keys().sort() on emit), (c) generated_at uses current time but generator_run_id is set from env (INTEGRITY_RUN_ID) when available so test harness can pin it. The non-deterministic generated_at is not a regression risk because the consumer (curmudgeon) only uses it for staleness comparison, not for content matching.
deliverable_5_script_design.performance_target: <5 seconds wall-clock on the current corpus (464 review files, 71 wins). Achievable: file reads are I/O-bound; fingerprint comparison is O(1) per item; total work is O(items × fields) ~ 71 × 7 ~ 500 ops. The actual bottleneck is JSON.parse on review files (~8MB total). Sequential parse is sufficient; no need for concurrency.

exit_codes.1: Input data missing (wins.json or reviews/ unreadable). Caller (integrity) escalates to Major.
exit_codes.2: Output write failed (disk full, permissions). Caller escalates to Major.
exit_codes.3: Internal error (parse exception, etc.). Caller escalates to Major and logs the stack trace.

deliverable_5_script_design.command_line_interface: node monitor/scripts/compute-drift-audit.js [--workspace /path/to/dome-model-review] [--top-n 30] [--drift-pct 20] [--out monitor/integrity/drift-audit.json]. Defaults match the schema thresholds. The --workspace flag matches digest-reviews.js for consistency.
deliverable_5_script_design.test_coverage: Add a test in test.js (or a sibling test file) that: (a) writes a tiny synthetic reviews/ directory + wins.json, (b) runs the script, (c) asserts the output matches a golden file. Cover at least: pre-tldr fingerprint with detail_verdict drift (the deep-tail PROP-019 case), verdict_changed item, new item with no review, item with comparable fingerprint and zero drift (should not appear in candidates), bespoke-fingerprint item (should be skipped, increment items_skipped_no_comparable_fingerprint).
deliverable_5_script_design.skeleton_notes: The above is a complete v1 sketch — runnable as-is once the operator authorizes. Key design points: (a) classify() implements PROP-019 reduced-set strictly; (b) tiebreak_score uses sentinel values (1e7, 1e6) for verdict_changed/new_item to make sort order obvious; (c) sections.json is read but not used in v1 (it's available for sha computation only); (d) priority queue cross-reference is best-effort — missing queue file is not an error.

deliverable_6_scheduling_change.recommendation: Absorb into integrity's existing daily 9am cron. No new scheduled task.
deliverable_6_scheduling_change.argument: Integrity already runs daily, has a structural-audit charter, and has a single context window in which all its checks fit. Adding a 5s deterministic step to that run is the lightest possible intervention. Counter-argument considered: a separate scheduled task at 6am (before integrity) could pre-populate the audit so integrity's daily check is a verification, not a generation. **Rejected** because it adds a scheduling artifact for no concrete benefit — the 72h gate already prevents wasted reruns, and the script's <5s runtime makes 'pre-populate' meaningless. Also rejected: integrity-mode picker (run audit on Mon/Thu only) — adds calendar coupling for negligible gain. The gate-driven approach self-heals if integrity skipped a day.
deliverable_6_scheduling_change.operator_visibility: Add a one-line entry to the daily-report briefing template (decider-side) showing 'drift_audit: {N candidates, generated YYYY-MM-DD}' so the operator sees the cadence at a glance without opening the file. This is a separate light edit to monitor/prompts/decider.md briefing template — flag it as an optional follow-up; not blocking PROP-020.

deliverable_7_first_run_disposition.recommendation: One per cycle, current cadence. No batching.
deliverable_7_first_run_disposition.argument: Curmudgeon's 'STOP after one item' rule (Step 8 of the per-run procedure) is a load-bearing invariant: it preserves Opus context budget, distributes work across runs for resilience, and makes the output cadence predictable for the decider. The deep-tail backlog (per PROP-019) is ~14 high-drift items + ~8 wider population. At 1-2 picks/day from change-detect, the backlog clears in 11-22 days. Batching the first run would: (a) violate the STOP rule for no structural reason, (b) compromise review depth on the high-magnitude items (these are the ones most overdue for fresh adversarial attention), (c) create an asymmetric 'first run blasts through 14 reviews then nothing for a week' signature that breaks downstream cadence assumptions in the digest pipeline and the decider.
deliverable_7_first_run_disposition.alternative_considered: Batch up to 5 deep-tail items in the first run, then resume singleton thereafter. Would clear the backlog faster but at the cost of (a)-(c) above. The directive's separate batching mechanism (Step 8a) is for *minor re-reviews following decider patches*, not for structural backlog clearance — extending it would conflate purposes.
deliverable_7_first_run_disposition.expected_first_run_pick: WIN-050 (vd 838%), per PROP-019's expected_pick_order. Subsequent runs work down the same list. After the first ~3-4 runs the candidate list is regenerated by integrity (72h gate) and the next batch of deep-tail items surfaces (some clear via reviews, some by data drift moving them out of the >20% threshold).
deliverable_7_first_run_disposition.no_priority_queue_push_needed: Confirmed — operator's lean on no queue push is correct (PROP-019 also recommended this). The candidate list IS the change-detect channel; pushing to the queue would conflate channels and obscure whether the structural fix actually delivers.

pre_split_baseline.wallclock: Operator's manual observation 2026-05-05 ~20:00Z: 'noticeably long' on the evaluate-which-item-to-pick phase. We don't have a precise number. Recommend: before landing this PROP, run 5 instrumented curmudgeon runs in pure Priority 3 mode and record the wall-clock from 'Step 0d entry' to 'review file write begins'. Median is the baseline.
pre_split_baseline.token_proxy: Curmudgeon currently reads ~462 review JSONs (8.3MB raw, ~2M tokens decompressed-equivalent) just to assemble fingerprints. Even if the LLM short-circuits much of this, the prompt structure obliges it to consider every reviewed item. Lower-bound estimate: 200-500K tokens of input on the change-detect phase alone, before the actual review begins.
pre_split_baseline.current_behavior_signature: Most change-detect picks come from the front-recent of the corpus (newly-changed items). Deep-tail items (28+ days old, highest drift) historically did NOT surface (PROP-019 evidence: 14 known high-drift items unseen for 28+ days).

a_curmudgeon_hot_path_meaningfully_faste.metric: Wall-clock from Step 0d entry to review file write begins.
a_curmudgeon_hot_path_meaningfully_faste.target: ≥50% reduction from baseline. Realistic expected reduction: ~75-90% — the LLM goes from scanning 462 files to reading one ~10KB JSON.
a_curmudgeon_hot_path_meaningfully_faste.disprove: If post-split runs show ≤20% reduction, the prompt edit didn't take effect — the LLM is still scanning despite the candidate list.

b_integrity_wallclock_includes_audit_ste.metric: Integrity report's runtime + presence of `drift_audit` block.
b_integrity_wallclock_includes_audit_ste.target: Audit step adds <10s wall-clock to integrity. drift_audit block in report shows status: 'refreshed' or 'fresh' on every run.
b_integrity_wallclock_includes_audit_ste.disprove: If audit step takes >30s, performance assumption broken; investigate. If `drift_audit` block missing, integrity prompt edit didn't land.

c_candidate_list_is_fresh_after_each_int.metric: monitor/integrity/drift-audit.json `generated_at` advances after each integrity run that triggered the gate.
c_candidate_list_is_fresh_after_each_int.target: Within the 72h gate window, generated_at refreshes; outside it, stale-skip is logged.
c_candidate_list_is_fresh_after_each_int.disprove: If generated_at hasn't moved in >7 days despite integrity running, the gate is broken or the script is silently failing.

d_curmudgeon_picks_deep_tail_in_magnitud.metric: Within the first 5 change-detect picks after PROP-020 lands, count picks from the WIN-050/028/009/036/060 deep-tail head.
d_curmudgeon_picks_deep_tail_in_magnitud.target: ≥3 of 5 picks come from this set in first 5 change-detect runs (allowing 2 for OBE-skips or queue-priority items intervening).
d_curmudgeon_picks_deep_tail_in_magnitud.disprove: If 0 of 5 first picks come from the deep tail despite the candidate list having them at the top, the cross-check or fallback logic is broken.

e_review_files_carry_drift_audit_metadat.metric: New change-detect review files include drift_audit_generated_at, drift_audit_candidate_index, and (if any) obe_skips fields.
e_review_files_carry_drift_audit_metadat.target: 100% of change-detect-triggered reviews have these fields after the prompt edit lands.
e_review_files_carry_drift_audit_metadat.disprove: If reviews lack these fields, the prompt edit didn't take effect or the LLM is taking the fallback path silently.

f_no_regression_in_priority_queue_servic.metric: Queue items continue to be picked first (Priority 1), with the same per-item review depth.
f_no_regression_in_priority_queue_servic.target: PROP-009 queue_id presence remains 100% on queue-sourced reviews.
f_no_regression_in_priority_queue_servic.disprove: If queue items get neglected in favor of candidate-list items, priority order has been broken.

g_correctness_preserved.metric: For 1-2 picks, manually verify the script's drift computation against an in-prompt scan. Tinker can do this in a Mode 3 audit.
g_correctness_preserved.target: Script's max_drift_pct matches in-prompt computation within 0.1%.
g_correctness_preserved.disprove: If discrepancy >0.1%, script has a bug (likely in the field-mapping between wins.json keys and fingerprint keys, e.g., detail_verdict_text→detail_verdict_length).

deliverable_8_regression_test.tinker_mode3_audit_addition: Add a tinker Mode 3 audit step (separate from this PROP — note for follow-up): periodically (every 2 weeks) run a synthetic in-prompt scan and compare to the latest drift-audit.json. Spot-check 3 candidates for max_drift_pct agreement. This catches script drift from the prompt's logical specification.

holistic_review_tldr_vs_detail_check.current_behavior: Holistic review (Priority 4) picks from the holistic_checks list in tracker.json. The 'TLDRs vs expanded content' check (item: 'Reader-path attack surface' or 'Consistency of tone') reads wins.json directly to compare TLDRs against detail content.
holistic_review_tldr_vs_detail_check.post_split_behavior: Unchanged. Holistic check does not depend on the change-detect candidate list. Priority 4 is a separate priority and runs only when Priority 3 produces no work (or when explicitly scheduled per holistic_checks rotation).
holistic_review_tldr_vs_detail_check.verification: Confirm by reading the holistic review procedure in curmudgeon-change-and-holistic.md §Holistic Reviews — it makes no mention of the candidate list.

spot_check_priority_5.current_behavior: Priority 5 picks a random previously-reviewed item and re-reviews it.
spot_check_priority_5.post_split_behavior: Unchanged. Spot-checks remain orthogonal to the candidate list. The candidate list is for drift-driven re-review; spot-checks are for catching drift the fingerprint doesn't catch.
spot_check_priority_5.alternative_considered_and_rejected: Source spot-check pool from the candidate list's non-drifting items (i.e., items the script audited and found stable). Rejected because (a) the script intentionally does not include non-drifting items in the candidates array (operator's lean from Q4); (b) the spot-check's value is partly its randomness — sourcing from a curated list defeats that.
spot_check_priority_5.future_extension_note: If operator later wants spot-check optimization, add a separate `monitor/integrity/spot-check-candidates.json` artifact with stable-but-aged items. Out of scope for PROP-020.

expanded_lenses_a_b_c.current_behavior: Advocate Mode (Lens A), Cross-WIN Consistency (Lens B), and Quantitative Verification (Lens C) apply to all change-driven reviews and spot-checks. They produce new fields in the review JSON.
expanded_lenses_a_b_c.post_split_behavior: Unchanged. The lenses operate on the *picked* item, not on the picking process. The candidate-list refactor is upstream of the picking decision.
expanded_lenses_a_b_c.verification: Confirm by reading curmudgeon-change-and-holistic.md §Expanded Review Lenses — they apply after item selection.

step_4_proposal_review_procedure.current_behavior: Reviewing target_type: proposal triggers a different review procedure (no prose to attack; review the proposal package's analytical questions).
step_4_proposal_review_procedure.post_split_behavior: Unchanged. Proposal reviews are queue-driven (Priority 1), not change-detect driven. The candidate list does not include EXP-* proposal items.
step_4_proposal_review_procedure.verification: Confirmed by script scope_in_v1: only WIN-NNN items are scored.

fingerprint_schema_evolution.current_behavior: Future schema additions to text_fingerprint (e.g., detail_extra_length) propagate as items get re-reviewed. PROP-019 reduced-set rule handles cross-version comparison.
fingerprint_schema_evolution.post_split_behavior: The script computes the current fingerprint from wins.json directly using a hardcoded field mapping. If a new fingerprint field is added to the curmudgeon prompt (e.g., finding_tldr_length), the script must also be updated to compute it. This is a tight coupling.
fingerprint_schema_evolution.mitigation: Document the field mapping in a comment block in compute-drift-audit.js. When a new fingerprint field lands in the curmudgeon prompt, also update the script in the same PR. Add a tinker Mode 3 audit step: 'verify compute-drift-audit.js field set matches the curmudgeon prompt's fingerprint schema'.
fingerprint_schema_evolution.alternative_considered_and_rejected: Have the script read the fingerprint schema from a separate config file (e.g., monitor/curmudgeon/fingerprint-schema.json) so prompt and script stay in sync via shared truth. Rejected for v1 because: (a) over-engineering for a 7-field schema; (b) adds another file with its own ownership/sync question. Revisit if the schema gains 5+ more fields.

items_without_canonical_fingerprint.current_behavior: Section reviews and bespoke-fingerprint reviews are scanned in-prompt by the LLM, which may or may not catch their drift depending on the LLM's interpretation of 'fingerprint' for non-canonical shapes.
items_without_canonical_fingerprint.post_split_behavior: These items are NOT in the candidate list. They fall through to the in-prompt fallback or to holistic review. This is a deliberate scope limit.
items_without_canonical_fingerprint.risk: Section drift might go uncaught between full-fallback events. Operator should accept this risk for v1; Phase 2 extends the script to sections.
items_without_canonical_fingerprint.mitigation: Integrity reports `items_skipped_no_comparable_fingerprint` count each run; if this count is >180 and rising, escalate to a Phase 2 extension.

tracker_json_interaction.current_behavior: tracker.json is updated when a review is written (Step 8 of curmudgeon's per-run).
tracker_json_interaction.post_split_behavior: Unchanged. The candidate list is read-only for curmudgeon; tracker writes happen as before, downstream of the pick.

deliverable_10_future_extensions.deep_tail_health_metric: Integrity could surface 'deep tail health' (count of items with last_review > 30 days AND comparable fingerprint AND no drift > threshold) as a daily metric. Catches drift that doesn't fire >20% but indicates aging coverage.
richer_signals_in_candidate_list: claim_conflicts (cross-WIN: this WIN's claim contradicts another WIN we recently re-reviewed) — would require a CWE-style cross-WIN graph; nontrivial
related_WIN_changed (this WIN's WIN-list link references another WIN whose detail just changed) — would require parsing WIN cross-references in detail_evidence
data_source_changed (this WIN cites a paper or dataset that integrity flagged as broken/redirected) — would require integrity to also tag external-link health on the candidate list
tldr_vs_detail_consistency_flag (this WIN's tldr_evidence and detail_evidence have substantially different lengths beyond the 4× ratio expected) — heuristic for tldr-rot
deliverable_10_future_extensions.phase_2_section_coverage: Extend the script to score section items (part1, part2b, ...) once a canonical section fingerprint shape is standardized. Probably needs to be section-by-section because sections.json has heterogeneous structure (h2-grouped subsections, not uniform shape per top-level key).
deliverable_10_future_extensions.phase_3_proposal_review_drift: EXP-NNN proposal reviews are also stale-prone (proposal package contents drift before integration). Could extend the audit to proposal packages once their fingerprint shape is standardized.
deliverable_10_future_extensions.tinker_audit_drift_audit_correctness: As noted in deliverable 8: every 2 weeks, tinker Mode 3 spot-checks the script's max_drift_pct against an in-prompt computation for 3 candidates. Catches script drift.
deliverable_10_future_extensions.decider_briefing_visibility: Add 'drift_audit: {N candidates, generated YYYY-MM-DD}' to the daily decider briefing — operator sees the audit cadence without opening the file. Light follow-up edit to decider.md briefing template; not blocking.

open_design_questions_addressed.question: Where should the candidate list live: monitor/integrity/drift-audit.json (writer's directory) or monitor/curmudgeon/drift-audit.json (reader's directory)?
open_design_questions_addressed.answer: monitor/integrity/drift-audit.json (writer's directory). Argument: file-ownership convention is 'file in writer's directory.' Existing precedent across monitor/integrity/ (report-*.json, workspace-sync-skips.jsonl, narrative-cite-audit-*.json, INTEGRITY-INCIDENT-*.json). Curmudgeon already reads from many directories (priority-queue.json, human-notes.json, tracker.json, reviews/) — a single read of integrity/drift-audit.json is no different. The 'reader's directory for readability' counter-argument is weak; it's a single read in Step 1 of Priority 3.

open_design_questions_addressed.question: Should the candidate list cap at top-N items (e.g. top 30) or include all items above the >20% threshold?
open_design_questions_addressed.answer: Cap large_rewrite + tldr_only at top 30 by max_drift_pct. Verdict_changed and new_item buckets are uncapped (always included). Argument: with only ~69 WINs scoring against canonical fingerprints in v1, 30 is enough for nearly any realistic drift state — if more than 30 items are drifting >20% simultaneously, we have systemic content churn that warrants an alert, not a longer list. Verdict-changed and new-items are categorically more important than any drift magnitude — they MUST be visible regardless of cap. Counter-argument: a deep-tail surge (e.g., schema migration causing all 40 pre-tldr WINs to surface) could exceed the cap. Mitigation: pre_tldr_schema_count in stats lets integrity flag this; operator can bump --top-n to 60 temporarily.

open_design_questions_addressed.question: What happens if integrity's audit step fails (script crashes, etc.)? Does curmudgeon notice and fall back, or silently use stale list?
open_design_questions_addressed.answer: Integrity emits a Major finding when the script fails (non-zero exit, missing output). The audit file becomes stale per its existing generated_at — curmudgeon's freshness check (>168h = 7d) catches this and falls back to in-prompt scan. Curmudgeon does NOT need direct integrity-failure detection; it observes 'list missing or stale' and falls back. Failure mode contract: separation of concerns. Integrity owns 'is the audit fresh?'; curmudgeon owns 'how do I act on the audit (or its absence)?'. This is identical to how curmudgeon currently handles a missing priority-queue.json (treats as empty queue, proceeds).

open_design_questions_addressed.question: Should the candidate list also contain non-drifting items (so curmudgeon has full visibility), or only drifting candidates?
open_design_questions_addressed.answer: Only drifting candidates. Argument: the candidate list's purpose is selecting the next item to review. Non-drifting items are not candidates — including them is dead weight that obscures the actual signal. The script's stats block carries non_drifting_count and items_with_comparable_fingerprint so the integrity report can surface deep-tail coverage health without polluting the list itself. If full-corpus visibility is needed for some other purpose (e.g., spot-check sourcing), that's a separate artifact. Counter-argument: 'curmudgeon might want to verify a specific non-drifting item's review was recent enough.' That's a one-off lookup in monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/ — no list needed.

open_design_questions_addressed.question: How does this interact with the priority queue? Should the candidate list cross-reference queue items?
open_design_questions_addressed.answer: Yes — each candidate carries `in_priority_queue: bool` and `queue_id: int|null`, computed at audit time by reading priority-queue.json. Argument: avoids the cycle 'curmudgeon Priority 1 services WIN-050 from queue → next cycle reads candidate list → looks up WIN-050 review → finds it's been re-reviewed → OBE-skip → wasted slot.' Annotating in_priority_queue lets curmudgeon's pick logic prefer candidates NOT in the queue (since queue items are already scheduled), reducing OBE-skip churn. Counter-argument: if the queue and candidate list disagree (queue has WIN-050 but the candidate is for a different drift on WIN-050), the cross-reference is misleading. Mitigation: the in_priority_queue flag is informational, not gating. Curmudgeon still applies the OBE-skip rule based on actual review file timestamps. Worst case: a pointless skip; best case: avoided redundant work.

non_goal_compliance.no_change_to_review_procedure: Confirmed — Steps 5-8 of Priority 3 are unchanged. Only Steps 1-2 are replaced. Step 4 prioritization is implicit in the script's pre-sorted output; the prompt no longer needs to express it.
non_goal_compliance.no_change_to_priority_queue: Confirmed — Priority 1 (queue) is untouched. The cross-reference is read-only.
non_goal_compliance.no_change_to_decider_or_consumers: Confirmed — digest-reviews.js, decider's review processing, and all downstream consumers are unaffected. The candidate list is curmudgeon-internal.
non_goal_compliance.no_realtime_watch_notify: Confirmed — periodic batch audit only. The 72h gate is the cadence floor.
non_goal_compliance.narrow_scope: Confirmed — exactly the change-detection scan input. No holistic batching, no spot-check changes, no expanded-lenses changes.

tradeoffs_and_other_side.main_gain: Curmudgeon hot-path drops from 'read 462 files, ~200-500K input tokens, ~minutes wall-clock' to 'read one ~10KB file, ~5K input tokens, ~seconds wall-clock' on the change-detect phase. At q4h × 6 runs/day this is meaningful Opus token spend.
tradeoffs_and_other_side.main_cost: Maintenance coupling between the script and the curmudgeon prompt's fingerprint schema. Future schema additions need synchronized updates.
tradeoffs_and_other_side.the_other_side_argument_against_this_spl: An LLM scanning 462 files in-context can sometimes catch nuance the deterministic script misses — e.g., it might notice 'this item's fingerprint is comparable but the underlying detail_evidence text was completely rewritten while staying the same length.' The script computes drift on lengths only, not semantics. Counter-mitigation: the script doesn't claim to replace the LLM's judgment, only to pre-rank. The LLM still does the actual review on the picked item. If the script misses a semantic-but-not-length-changing item, the holistic review (Priority 4) and spot-check (Priority 5) provide the safety net.
tradeoffs_and_other_side.the_other_side_argument_against_falling_: If the audit ran 24h ago and the queue has been firing furiously, the top of the candidate list could be all-OBE while items 7+ are still valid. Falling through after 3 wastes the in-prompt scan when the 4th candidate would have been the right pick. **Mitigation:** verdict-changed and new-item entries (the highest-priority buckets) are very unlikely to be OBE in 24h. The fallback is structurally identical to pre-PROP-020 work; it costs nothing relative to the baseline. If operator finds OBE-cap=3 too aggressive in practice, bumping to 5 is a one-token edit.
tradeoffs_and_other_side.the_other_side_argument_for_doing_nothin: The current state works — the deep tail surfaces eventually as PROP-019's reduced-set rule clears it (~14-22 days). Why add architectural complexity for an issue that resolves in <30 days? **Counter:** PROP-019 fixes the misrouting bug, but the underlying scan cost remains every cycle. PROP-019 ≠ PROP-020. PROP-019 makes the scan correct; PROP-020 makes the scan cheap. Both add value; both can land independently.
tradeoffs_and_other_side.the_other_side_argument_for_real_time_wa: Watching wins.json for changes and recomputing drift per write would give always-fresh candidate lists. **Rejected by directive non-goal**, but worth noting: real-time watch buys ~72h of freshness at the cost of (a) more complex infrastructure, (b) more failure modes (watcher dies silently). Periodic batch is simpler and the curmudgeon's q4h cadence is already a quasi-batch interval. Real-time is over-engineering for this problem.

estimated_impact.curmudgeon_hot_path_token_spend: ~95% reduction on the change-detect phase. Total per-run reduction ~50-70% (because the actual review still consumes the bulk of the budget; only the 'pick which item' phase shrinks).
estimated_impact.integrity_wall_clock: +5-10 seconds per integrity run that triggers the gate (every ~3 days). Negligible.
estimated_impact.deep_tail_clearance: Same as PROP-019 (11-22 days). PROP-020 doesn't accelerate it, but it makes future deep tails surface predictably without LLM cost.
estimated_impact.structural_resilience: Permanent. Future fingerprint schema additions can be propagated to the script in a single line; future scope expansions (sections, proposals) extend the same script without prompt changes.
estimated_impact.review_quality: No change. The actual review depth, lenses, and citation discipline are unaffected — only the picker is replaced.

completion_criteria_check.schema_concrete: Yes — example entries cover verdict_changed (WIN-022), new_item (WIN-072), and pre-tldr-schema large_rewrite (WIN-050).
completion_criteria_check.prompt_diffs_drafted: Yes — both integrity (§7g new section) and curmudgeon (Steps 1-4 replacement) drafts included with before/after.
completion_criteria_check.fallback_clear: Yes — drift-audit.json missing/malformed/>168h-old/sha-mismatch all route to in-prompt scan; details specified.
completion_criteria_check.obe_skip_policy_argued: Yes — recommend 3, with counter-argument and bump-to-5 escape hatch.
completion_criteria_check.open_questions_addressed: Yes — all 5 directive open questions have explicit answers with reasoning.
completion_criteria_check.directive_status_field_to_set: completed (this proposal authoring run satisfies the directive).

why_human_needed: Operator must (1) authorize implementation (the directive is propose-only); (2) confirm the path choice (monitor/integrity/drift-audit.json); (3) confirm the cadence (72h gate); (4) confirm the OBE-cap (3); (5) confirm v1 scope is WIN-only and section coverage is Phase 2. Tinker authored the proposal; tinker did NOT modify monitor/prompts/curmudgeon.md, monitor/prompts/reference/curmudgeon-change-and-holistic.md, monitor/prompts/structure-integrity.md, or create monitor/scripts/compute-drift-audit.js per the directive's do_not_implement clause.
verification_pattern: test -f monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-020-drift-audit-split.json && grep -q '"verification_pattern"' monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-020-drift-audit-split.json && echo FIXED || echo STILL_BROKEN
post_implementation_verification_pattern: test -f monitor/integrity/drift-audit.json && test -f monitor/scripts/compute-drift-audit.js && grep -q 'monitor/integrity/drift-audit.json' monitor/prompts/reference/curmudgeon-change-and-holistic.md && grep -q 'compute-drift-audit' monitor/prompts/structure-integrity.md && echo IMPLEMENTED || echo NOT_IMPLEMENTED
cross_references: commit 71be960 — drift-magnitude tiebreak (script implements magnitude-sorted output)
build-scripts/digest-reviews.js (precedent: deterministic Node script that pre-digests review state for the decider)
monitor/scripts/audit-narrative-citations.js (precedent: deterministic Node audit script in monitor/scripts/)
